data.title: Brazil New Vehicle Sales Grow for the 2nd Month
data.description:
New vehicle sales in Brazil increased by 8.1% in May 2025, compared to the previous month, reaching 214,700 units, after a 6.7% increase in April.

Despite still-high interest rates and uncertainties about decisions and retaliations regarding US tariffs, the market has shown that it remains resilient, supported by a recovery in the supply of credit and firm demand from rental companies.

Meanwhile, vehicle exports increased by 11.3% in April, totaling 51,500 units compared to the previous month, with a highlight being the consistent growth in sales to Argentina.

On the other hand, the sector is sounding the alarm with the increasing entry of Chinese vehicles into the Brazilian market, favored by significantly lower taxation compared to other countries, which has been generating a worrying competitive distortion.

data.title: IperionX Gets US Department of Defense Contract; Shares Rise
data.description:
IperionX said Thursday it has received a small business innovation research phase 3 contract from the US Department of Defense to achieve "Low-Cost Domestic Titanium for Defense Applications" in the US.

The contract enables the firm to receive task order funding from the department agencies up to a total of $99 million, it said.

"This award recognizes IperionX's readiness to deliver strategic titanium components for US defense applications," the firm said.

Shares were up almost 20% in recent trading.

data.title: CRACKER BARREL OLD COUNTRY STORE, INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Cracker Barrel Old Country Store, Inc., a renowned American chain of combined restaurant and gift stores, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational activities, showcasing a mix of growth and challenges in a dynamic market environment.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $821.1 million, increased by 0.5% for the third quarter of 2025 compared to the same period in 2024, driven by a 1.0% increase in comparable store restaurant sales.
  • Gross Profit: The cost of goods sold was $247.3 million, representing 30.1% of total revenue for the third quarter of 2025.
  • Operating Income: $14.9 million, reflecting an improvement from an operating loss of $19.3 million in the third quarter of 2024, primarily due to lower impairment and store closing costs.
  • Net Income: $12.6 million, compared to a net loss of $9.2 million in the third quarter of 2024, attributed to improved operating income and a lower effective tax rate.
  • Net Income Per Share: Basic $0.56, Diluted $0.56, compared to a net loss per share of $(0.41) in the third quarter of 2024, reflecting the turnaround in net income.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported revenue from two primary segments: restaurant and retail. Restaurant revenue accounted for 82.7% of total revenue in the third quarter of 2025, while retail revenue made up 17.3%.
  • Sales Units: As of May 02, 2025, the company operated 658 Cracker Barrel stores and 70 Maple Street Biscuit Company (MSBC) locations. The net change in units for the third quarter included the opening of one Cracker Barrel and one MSBC location.
  • Comparable Store Sales: For the third quarter of 2025, comparable store restaurant sales increased by 1.0%, driven by a 6.6% increase in average check per guest, despite a 5.6% decrease in guest traffic. Comparable store retail sales decreased by 3.8%.
  • Operational Challenges: The company faced challenges such as lower consumer demand due to macroeconomic factors, including inflationary pressures and higher consumer debt levels, impacting guest traffic.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to focus on its strategic transformation plan, which includes refining the brand, enhancing the menu, evolving the store and guest experience, winning in digital and off-premise channels, and elevating the employee experience.
  • New Initiatives: The company is testing remodel prototypes and expects to complete 25-30 remodels and 25-30 store refreshes in 2025. Additionally, it is leveraging technology through the Cracker Barrel Rewards loyalty program to drive customer engagement.
  • Commodity and Wage Inflation: The company expects commodity inflation to be in the mid 2% range and wage inflation to be in the mid 2% range for 2025.
  • Employee Experience: The company is upgrading training and development programs and simplifying job roles to improve the employee experience.
  • Strategic Transformation Plan: The company has modified its capital allocation policy and expects to increase capital expenditures to approximately $600,000 to $700,000 over the three-year period from 2025 to 2027, focusing on maintenance, remodels, and technology improvements.

SEC Filing: CRACKER BARREL OLD COUNTRY STORE, INC [ CBRL ] - 10-Q - Jun. 05, 2025

data.title: Past is prologue: Downbeat data ahead of May payrolls
data.description:
Key points:
  • Main US indexes up; Nasdaq in the lead
  • Info tech up most among S&P sector gainers; staples biggest laggard
  • Euro STOXX 600 index up ~0.1%; ECB cuts 25 bps
  • Dollar off; gold dips; bitcoin up; crude gains ~1%
  • US 10-Year Treasury yield rises to ~4.39%

PAST IS PROLOGUE: DOWNBEAT DATA AHEAD OF MAY PAYROLLS

A spate of soft data on Thursday seemed to set the table for a disappointing May employment report.

Last week, 247,000 U.S. workers joined the queue outside the unemployment office (USJOB=ECI), an unexpected 3.3% weekly increase, overshooting consensus by 8,400.

That's the highest initial claims print since October, and the underlying trend, as expressed by the four-week moving average of initial claims, now has an upward bias, suggesting layoffs are on the upswing.

While last week's claims data falls outside the survey period for the May jobs report, it's the latest in a string of soft labor market indicators.

Speaking of which, a separate report from executive outplacement firm Challenger Gray & Christmas (CGC) (USCHAL=ECI) showed that in May, corporate America announced it would lay off 93,816 workers, or 11% fewer than in April, but a 47% increase over May 2024.

From January through May, 696,309 job cuts have been announced. That's 80% more than the 385,859 announced in the first five months of last year.

"Tariffs, funding cuts, consumer spending, and overall economic pessimism are putting intense pressure on companies’ workforces," writes Andrew Challenger, CGC's senior vice president. "Companies are spending less, slowing hiring, and sending layoff notices."

So far this year, the government - largely as a result of billionaire Elon Musk's DOGE efforts - has cut 284,827 jobs. That's 41% of total year-to-date layoffs.

Ongoing jobless claims (USJOBN=ECI), reported on a one-week lag, essentially held steady at 1.904 million, or a scant 6,000 fewer than analysts expected. The number remains elevated and supports recent consumer survey data suggesting laid off workers are finding it increasingly difficult to find a replacement gig.

"The latest jobless claims data are signaling looser labor market conditions, with an uptick in initial claims suggesting layoffs may be rising while the elevated level of continuing claims confirms it is difficult for unemployed workers to find new jobs," says Nancy Vanden Houten, lead economist at Oxford Economics.

The trade gap (USTBAL=ECI), or the difference in the value of goods and services imported to the U.S. and those exported abroad, narrowed in April by 55.5% to $61.6 billion.

The reading is 12% narrower than economist predictions, notching the lowest trade deficit since November 2023.

"The economy has essentially hit pause on discretionary imports and is now working off inventories as businesses and consumers delay spending and wait for clarity on tariffs,” says Nationwide Financial Markets Economist Oren Klachkin.

Under the hood, Trump's erratic tariff policies are starting to sting; a 3.0% increase in exports was handily obliterated by a 16.3% drop in imports, which account for the lion's share of the United States' total international trade.

Here's a look at plunging imports from three of the United States' biggest trading partners: Canada, China and Mexico.

Turning back to the jobs market, the Labor Department revised its first-quarter labor cost (USLCA=ECI) and productivity (USPROR=ECI) growth data, bumping the former up to 6.6% from 5.7% and slashing the latter to a 1.5% decline from the originally stated 0.8% dip.

Both numbers are expressed in quarterly annualized percent change.

All of this is prologue to the May employment report, due Friday morning. Economists polled by Reuters expect it to show the U.S. economy added 130,000 jobs last month, leaving the unemployment rate unchanged at 4.2%.

(Stephen Culp)

*****

data.title: REG-Prospectus updates
data.description:
Lysaker, 5 June 2025

The prospectuses have been updated as the document structure between SKAGEN funds and Storebrand funds have been aligned with the latter. There are no material changes, but all prospectuses now follow same structure consisting of:

  • Two fund specific pages (pages 1-2) containing information such as investment objective, benchmark and information about active shareclasses
  • Following six pages (pages 3-8) containing general prospectus information such as unitholders’ rights and obligations, subscriptions and redemptions, custodian, tax rules, management company, the Board, outsourcing, etc., and hence, this part is common for all the funds.
  • Following a variable amount of pages (from page 9) containing the fund’s articles of association.
  • Finally, follows the fund’s SFDR Disclosure, annex II for article 8 funds and annex III for article 9 funds.

General updates of the Prospectuses

In addition to the above, the Prospectuses have also been subject to general updates, including Board Members and renumerations of the Board and CEO.

The Prospectuses can be found herein, or at www.storebrand.com and www.skagenfunds.com.

Regards

Storebrand Asset Management AS

data.title: Microbot Medical Selected in Preliminary List for Russell Microcap Index Inclusion
data.description:
Microbot Medical said Thursday it was selected for preliminary inclusion in the Russell Microcap Index.

The newly reconstituted indexes based on the preliminary list of additions will be effective after the market closes on June 27 and will be part of the Russell indexes reconstitution on June 30, according to a statement.

Shares were down more than 2% in recent trading.

data.title: Coveo Solutions Outperform Rating, C$8.50 Price Target, Confirmed at National Bank of Canada
data.description:
National Bank of Canada on Wednesday maintained its outperform rating and C$8.50 price target on the shares of Coveo Solutions .

The bank said it hosted Coveo Solutions' Chief Financial Officer, Brandon Nussey, as part of its Annual NBF Quebec Conference and provided key takeaways.

The bank noted that incumbent solutions, such as Salesforce, are generally sufficient for smaller businesses with less complex data to address their needs, but large enterprises with complex data sets typically have vast amounts of unstructured data across several vendor systems.

Coveo's edge, noted the bank, comes from its ability to collectively analyze the disparate enterprise data, allowing it "to surface meaningful ROI (return on investment)" for its customers.

"Enterprise software spend, particularly around AI, became challenged with the proliferation of ChatGPT as companies paused to assess the rapidly evolving technology and the value associated with related AI products," said the bank, adding that Coveo has been able to demonstrate tangible or measurable results while companies have an increased understanding of the value proposition, which is leading to more conversions.

The National Bank pointed out that Coveo's bookings grew more than 50% year-over-year across the second half of its fiscal year 2025 and that the company is targeting a reacceleration in revenue growth to approximately 16% on its Core Platform in FY26 from 11% in FY25. Due to this, the bank added, the company believes the current strong ROI with a payback period within two years supports an increase in spend on Sales & Marketing (primarily) to take advantage of the current returns.

The bank also mentioned that Coveo's partnership with Salesforce, SAP and Shopify are progressing at different stages.

"All in, we continue to believe Coveo remains one of the few direct plays on AI in our coverage universe where bookings and pipeline appear to be building momentum and setting up for accelerating growth in FY26," added the bank.

data.title: Seven Church of England priests face disciplinary process over abuse scandal
data.description:
Key points:
  • Three others named in safeguarding report face no further action
  • All ten cases were independently reviewed - Church statement
  • Safeguarding crisis forced out Archbishop Justin Welby in 2024

Seven Church of England priests, including a former senior bishop, will face disciplinary proceedings over safeguarding failures in the first such move since Archbishop Justin Welby was forced to stand down last year over a child abuse scandal.

Former Archbishop George Carey, who was in February named in a list of 10 historic cases that would be considered by an independent judicial process, would not face any further action, the Church also said in an update published on Thursday.

The Church of England - central to 85 million Anglicans worldwide - has been in crisis over safeguarding the vulnerable since a November report said ex-leader Welby had taken insufficient action to stop a prolific abuser.

In February it identified 10 cases that should be independently re-examined because they had happened too long ago to be considered by the Church's usual processes.

Former Bishop of Durham Paul Butler - who held one of the most senior bishoprics in the Church from 2014 to 2024 - is among those facing disciplinary proceedings. That process could result in penalties such as a permanent ban from ministry.

Butler was responsible for safeguarding oversight in 16,000 Anglican churches in the country from 2010-2016.

The proceedings against him and others form the next stage of the Church's efforts to restore trust following November's review. Welby, who stepped down within a month of the review's publication, is not facing disciplinary procedures.

While the Church has repeated its commitment to taking seriously its response to the review, its governing body stopped short of backing a fully independent safeguarding model to handle complaints in February.

data.title: Amazon settles State Farm lawsuit alleging it stole elder care technology
data.description:
Amazon has settled a lawsuit by insurance company State Farm accusing the e-commerce giant of copying its patented technology to develop elder care services for Amazon's virtual assistant Alexa, according to a filing in Delaware federal court.

The companies asked the court on Wednesday to dismiss the case with prejudice, which means it cannot be refiled. State Farm said in a statement on Thursday that the case had been settled under confidential terms.

Spokespersons and attorneys for Amazon did not immediately respond to a request for comment on Thursday.

Bloomington, Illinois-based State Farm sued Amazon in 2022, in what the insurance company said was its first-ever patent infringement lawsuit. According to the complaint, State Farm met with Amazon in 2019 and 2020 about incorporating its Sundial senior-care assistance technology into Alexa-enabled devices.

The lawsuit had accused Amazon of copying patented Sundial features, including its "circle of friends" for coordinating caregiver responsibilities, a chatbot for seniors to interact with the platform, and check-in and monitoring services.

Amazon had denied the allegations and argued that the patents were invalid.

The case is State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Co. v. Amazon.com Inc, U.S. District Court for the District of Delaware, No. 1:22-cv-01447.

For State Farm: Michael De Vries, Adam Alper, Akshay Deoras, Kat Li and Leslie Schmidt of Kirkland & Ellis

For Amazon: Doug Lumish of Latham & Watkins, Adam Greenfield of White & Case

Read more:

State Farm lawsuit says Amazon stole elder-care technology

Following this purchase, the Company's issued share capital consists of 36,143,690 ordinary shares of 25p each, the total number of shares in treasury is 1,577,500 and the total number of voting rights in the Company is 34,566,190.

data.title: REG - National Grid PLC - Scrip Dividend for 2024/25 Final Dividend
data.description:
RNS Number : 6742L National Grid PLC 05 June 2025  

5 June 2025

National Grid plc ('National Grid' or the 'Company')

Scrip Dividend for 2024/25 Final Dividend

National Grid today announces the scrip dividend reference price for the 2024/25 final dividend. A timetable is provided below.

For ordinary shareholders, the scrip dividend reference price for the 2024/25 final dividend is 1,040.40 pence. This is calculated as the average closing mid-market price of an ordinary share for the five dealing days commencing with, and including, the ordinary share ex-dividend date.

For American Depositary Receipt ('ADR') holders, the scrip ADR reference price for the 2024/25 final dividend is US$70.3102. This is calculated by multiplying the scrip dividend reference price above by five (as there are five ordinary shares underlying each ADR) and by the average US$ rate for the equivalent dates.  

The terms and conditions of the Scrip Dividend Scheme are available on the Investors section of the Company's website and from Equiniti (0800 169 7775).

data.title: Irish corporate tax falls sharply as one-off boost falls away
data.description:
Growth in Ireland's underlying tax revenue for the first five months of the year slowed to 3.6% after a sharp dip in corporate tax receipts in May that the finance ministry said was due to one-off factors from a year ago falling away.

Ireland has collected record levels of tax each year since 2021, mainly due to a surge in corporate receipts, but these were 9.4% lower year-to-date at the end of May, excluding proceeds from a ruling last year related to Apple back taxes.

Corporate tax receipts in May, one of the first major payment months of the year, fell to 2.5 billion euros ($2.86 billion) from 3.6 billion euros a year ago, the data from the finance ministry showed on Thursday.

Ireland collected between 2.4 billion euros and 2.9 billion euros in the corresponding month in 2021, 2022 and 2023.

The finance ministry forecasts corporate receipts, excluding the Apple proceeds, to post a rare annual decline this year and fall back by 2%. Growth elsewhere is expected to push overall receipts 3% higher on an underlying basis, it said last month.

Growth of 4.5% in income tax receipts and 5.5% in VAT so far this year kept that forecast on track at the end of May.

The corporate tax haul, underpinned mainly by a small number of foreign multinational firms, has handed Ireland the healthiest public finances in Europe.

An underlying exchequer surplus of 0.7 billion euros in May was broadly similar to a year ago.

($1 = 0.8728 euros)

data.title: AlTi Global Executives Sell Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameRelationshipTypeAmountSEC Filing
Graham Colleen AChief Legal, Compl & Risk OfcrSell$79,580Form 4
Tiedemann MichaelChief Executive OfficerSell$146,427Form 4
Connell BrookePres, US Wealth MgmtSell$74,473Form 4
Moran Kevin P.President and COOSell$98,926Form 4

Several top executives at AlTi Global have recently sold shares of the company's Class A Common Stock, according to SEC Form 4 filings. On June 3, 2025, Graham Colleen A, the Chief Legal, Compliance, and Risk Officer, sold 22,934 shares at a price of $3.47 each, totaling $79,580. Following this transaction, Graham directly owns 41,960 shares of AlTi Global.

Michael Tiedemann, the CEO of AlTi Global, sold 42,198 shares at the same price, totaling $146,427. Post-sale, Tiedemann directly owns 541,143 shares and has indirect ownership of 137,198 shares through various trusts and entities, including MGT 2012 DE Trust, CHT Fam Tst Ar 3rd fbo MGT, and Chauncey Close, LLC, though he disclaims beneficial ownership except for any pecuniary interest.

Connell Brooke, President of US Wealth Management, sold 21,462 shares at $3.47 per share, for a total of $74,473. Brooke's direct ownership in the company now stands at 216,388 shares.

Kevin P. Moran, President and COO, sold 28,509 shares at the same price, with the transaction amounting to $98,926. Following the sale, Moran directly owns 60,311 shares of AlTi Global.

data.title: ECB's Easing Cycle Is Coming to An End, Says Berenberg
data.description:
While the European Central Bank's (ECB) monetary policy statement contained no new information, President Christine Lagarde's comments during the press conference suggested that Thursday's 25bp reduction in the deposit rate to 2.0% could be the last interest rate cut of this cycle, said Brenberg.

Lagarde repeatedly emphasized that interest rates are now in a good position to navigate the current uncertain environment. This supports Berenberg's view that the European Central Bank has finished cutting rates, although some fine-tuning may remain, depending on future developments, including in the trade conflict between the United States and the European Union.

During the press conference, President Lagarde sought to convey the impression that the situation is as much under control as possible, given continued high economic uncertainty. Lagarde reiterated on multiple occasions that, following Thursday's interest rate cut, the ECB is now "in a good position to navigate the uncertain conditions that will be coming up."

Lagarde also stated that "we are reaching the end of the monetary policy cycle." In addition, the ECB anticipates that core inflation will come in at 1.9% in both 2026 and 2027. President Lagarde remarked on this projection, stating that "core inflation is hardly moving and close to target."

The ECB recognizes the present state of exceptional uncertainty and will continue to follow a data-dependent and meeting-by-meeting approach. Much will depend on the ongoing trade dispute between the U.S. and the EU.

The bank anticipate that the two parties will reach a preliminary agreement by early July at the latest. If so, a decrease in uncertainty, coupled with increased fiscal spending towards the end of the year — particularly in Germany — will enable the eurozone economy to regain momentum later this year, following a soft patch now.

As a consequence, Berenberg doesn't expect the ECB to lower its deposit rate below 2% unless the trade war with the U.S. escalates and the economy deteriorates as a result.

Against a backdrop of high uncertainty, ECB staff also examined how different trade policies could impact growth and inflation under various alternative scenarios. According to this scenario analysis, a further escalation of trade tensions over the coming months would result in growth and inflation being below the baseline projections. By contrast, if trade tensions were resolved with a benign outcome, growth and, to a lesser extent, inflation would be higher than in the baseline projections.

The ECB has lowered its headline inflation forecast for this year from 2.3% to 2.0% and for 2025 from 1.9% to 1.6%. The downward revisions compared with the March projections, by 0.3bp for both 2025 and 2026, mainly reflect lower assumptions for energy prices and a stronger euro. However, the ECB revised its forecast for core inflation up by 20bps to 2.4% for this year and down by 10bps to 1.9% for next year.

As such, the ECB now expects core inflation to fall below the 2% target as early as next year, rather than in 2027. The ECB has left its gross domestic product growth forecasts almost unchanged compared with those in March.

For the current year, the ECB believes that the stronger-than-expected growth in Q1 will be offset by weaker prospects for the rest of the year. It expects the uncertainty surrounding trade policies to have an impact on business investment and exports, particularly in the very short term.

However, rising government investment in defense and infrastructure will provide increasing support for growth further down the line, added Berenberg.

data.title: Sunlight Simplify and Preverity Announce Strategic Integration
data.description:
ORLANDO, Fla. and NASHVILLE, Tenn., June 05, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Sunlight Simplify, a leading provider of cloud-based, no-code and low-code policy administration solutions for insurance carriers and managing general agents (MGAs) and Preverity, Inc., the nation’s leader in clinical risk solutions, are excited about this new partnership aiming to revolutionize how insurers manage policy lifecycles by embedding real-time medical and billing intelligence directly into underwriting, claims processing, and policy servicing workflows.

“This partnership reflects our commitment to innovation and automation in insurance operations,” said Bernadette Leh, President of Sunlight Simplify. “By connecting Sunlight Simplify with verified medical and billing information, our customers can make faster, more informed decisions, ultimately improving service quality and operational efficiency.”

Preverity brings deep domain expertise and a robust network of data sources, delivering accurate, real-time medical records and billing data through a secure API framework. By embedding this intelligence into the Sunlight Simplify ecosystem, insurers gain a powerful tool to reduce fraud, accelerate underwriting, and simplify audits.

“We’re excited to work with Sunlight Simplify to help carriers modernize their approach to policy management,” said Gene Boerger, President & COO of Preverity. “This integration empowers insurance providers with the data transparency they need to stay competitive in a digital-first marketplace.”

About Sunlight Simplify

Sunlight Simplify is a cloud based, no-code & low-code, Policy Administration software solution for Insurance Carriers and MGAs. The enterprise suite is tailored to support the specific requirements of the Medical Professional Liability Insurance line of business. The highly flexible, multi-language, multi-currency configurable solution allows for quick implementation across states, territories and countries.

Contacts:

Martin Kowal

Sunlight Simplify

mkowal@sunlightsolutions.com

708-668-3794

About Preverity

Preverity, Inc. is the nation’s pre-eminent Insurtech provider of technology and advanced analytics focused on improving patient safety and advancing clinical risk management for healthcare systems and medical malpractice insurance carriers. With the largest risk management repository of medical and pharmacy claims data in the US, Preverity provides timely and accurate intelligence on clinical activities benchmarked against national, regional, and system standards. Learn more at www.preverity.com.

Preverity PSO, LLC is a federally listed Patient Safety Organization and wholly owned subsidiary of Preverity, Inc., focused on improving patient outcomes and enhancing overall safety in healthcare delivery. Visit www.preveritypso.com to learn more.

Contacts:

Gene Boerger

Preverity

gene.boerger@preverity.com

615-260-3925

data.title: The observation status for Qlosr Group AB (publ) is updated
data.description:
Nasdaq Stockholm ABFirst North Growth Market - Equities

The observation status for Qlosr Group AB (publ) is updated

On February 28, 2025, Qlosr Group AB (publ) (the “Company”) was givenobservation status with reference to information that the Company had divestedall its significant subsidiaries and material adverse uncertainty in respect ofthe Company's financial position.

Today, June 5, 2025, the Company disclosed that Nasdaq Stockholm AB (the“Exchange) had informed the Company that, unless the Company have informed theExchange of how it will fulfill the listing requirement of having ongoingbusiness operations before August 29, 2025, Nasdaq Stockholm AB may initiate aprocess for delisting the Company.

The rules of Nasdaq First North Growth Market state thatan issuer's financialinstruments may be given observation status if circumstances exist justifyingthe removal of the issuer's financial instruments from trading on Nasdaq FirstNorth Growth Market pursuant to item 8.2.7 of the rulebook.

With reference to the above, Nasdaq Stockholm AB decides to update theobservation status for the shares in Qlosr Group AB (publ) (QLOSR B, ISIN codeSE0018169195, order book ID 258945).

For further information about this exchange notice please contact Enforcement &Investigations, telephone +46 8 405 70 50.https://view.news.eu.nasdaq.com/view?id=b0b57c3048eae35f790286b841041096e&lang=en&src=omxlink

data.title: Descartes Systems Is Maintained at Outperform by RBC Capital
European Equities Traded in the US as American Depositary Receipts Rise Thursday
data.description:
European equities traded in the US as American depositary receipts were advancing on Thursday morning, up 0.7% to 1,476.59 on the S&P Europe Select ADR Index.

From continental Europe, the gainers were led by healthcare company Grifols and financial services provider Banco Santander , which rose 3.2% and 1.9% respectively.

They were followed by steel manufacturer ArcelorMittal , which added 1.7%, and semiconductor company ASML , which increased 1.5%.

The decliners from continental Europe were led by biotech company Evaxion and cybersecurity firm Wisekey (WKEY), which fell 4.3% and 2.2% respectively.

They were followed by advertising company Criteo , down 2%.

From the UK and Ireland, the gainers were led by biotech firm Autolus Therapeutics and cigarette manufacturer British American Tobacco , which rose 3.6% and 2.6% respectively.

The decliners from the UK and Ireland were led by biotech firm Centessa Pharmaceuticals and beverage giant Diageo , which lost 7.8% and 3.9% respectively.

data.title: Vermilion Energy gains after US assets sale, raises annual production forecast
data.description:
** Shares of Canadian gas producer Vermilion Energy rise 1.2% to C$9.24

** Company to sell its U.S. assets for C$120 million ($87.91 million)

** Adds that using the proceeds it will repay debt and complete its exit from the U.S. market

** Company also raises its annual production forecast to between 117,000 and 122,000 barrels of oil equivalent per day (boepd) from 84,000 to 88,000 boepd earlier

** It also cut its 2025 capital budget plan by about C$100 million to between C$630 million and C$660 million, reflecting the removal of expenses related to the assets divested in Saskatchewan and the U.S.

** Sale transaction is expected to close in the third quarter

** Including session's moves, VET down 32% YTD

($1 = 1.3650 Canadian dollars)

data.title: Birdie Win Corp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Birdie Win Corp, a company specializing in Personal Financial Literacy Seminars, has released its Form 10-Q report for the quarter ending April 30, 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational activities, showcasing both growth and challenges faced during the period.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $5,000 for the three months ended April 30, 2025, consistent with the same period in 2024, generated from one Personal Financial Literacy Seminar.
  • Revenue: $25,000 for the nine months ended April 30, 2025, up from $20,000 in the same period in 2024, due to an increase in the number of seminars provided.
  • Operating Expenses: $11,855 for the three months ended April 30, 2025, up from $9,606 in the same period in 2024, primarily due to increased consultancy fees.
  • Operating Expenses: $31,033 for the nine months ended April 30, 2025, down from $36,719 in the same period in 2024, mainly due to a decrease in allowance for doubtful accounts and bookkeeping fees.
  • Net Loss: $6,855 for the three months ended April 30, 2025, compared to $4,606 in the same period in 2024.
  • Net Loss: $6,033 for the nine months ended April 30, 2025, compared to $16,719 in the same period in 2024, reflecting improved cost management.
  • Net Loss Per Common Share – Basic and Diluted: (0.00) for both the three and nine months ended April 30, 2025 and 2024, indicating no change in per-share loss.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company generated revenue from providing Personal Financial Literacy Seminars (PFL Seminars) to participants, with a total of five seminars conducted during the nine months ended April 30, 2025, compared to four seminars in the same period in 2024.
  • Geographical Performance: The company's operations are focused on providing services in Hong Kong and Malaysia, with all revenue for the nine months ended April 30, 2025, being generated from these regions.
  • Sales Units: For the nine months ended April 30, 2025, the company conducted five Personal Financial Literacy Seminars, an increase from four seminars in the same period in 2024, indicating a growth in service delivery.
  • Operational Expenses: The company experienced a decrease in general and administrative expenses for the nine months ended April 30, 2025, primarily due to a reduction in allowance for doubtful accounts and bookkeeping fees.
  • Customer Concentration: The company had a single customer accounting for 100% of its revenue for both the three and nine months ended April 30, 2025, highlighting a significant reliance on a single revenue source.
  • Management Changes: The company underwent several management changes, with new appointments including Shiyong Zhao as CEO and Chairman, and Fengjun Wang and Yidong Bao as Directors, which may influence future strategic directions.
  • Future Outlook: The company continues to focus on expanding its seminar services in Hong Kong and Malaysia, aiming to improve financial literacy among individuals and families in these regions.

SEC Filing: Birdie Win Corp [ BRWC ] - 10-Q - Jun. 05, 2025
